name: Penciljam Bengaluru at HAL Heritage Centre and Aerospace Museum
description:
Our sketching session at the HAL Heritage Centre and Aerospace Museum takes us into the story of India’s journey through aviation and aerospace innovation.

Established in 2001 by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), India’s first aerospace museum traces over six decades of aircraft design, engineering, and flight through historic aircraft, helicopters, aero engines, simulators, and archival displays.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.
https://penciljam.com/main/community/

If this is your first time and would like future update on events, and more. Please register here.
Once you arrive at HAL

 	Buy a ticket at the entrance
 	You can carry an A4/A5 or smaller sketchbook and portable dry material kit to draw within exhibit space.
 	Choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable - be mindful of not blocking the path of visitors to the museum.

Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface
 	Pencils or any medium you would like to try out
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground
 	Water bottle and snacks

How to get there
Please follow the route Map.

location.name: HAL Heritage Centre and Aerospace Museum

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at Aurobindo Society
description:
Built in 1905 by Gangadhar Chetty, a prominent businessman, the house later became the residence of Colonel Jai Prithvi Bahadur Singh, the Raja of Bajhang, in 1921. He renamed it Jaya Bhavan and used it as a space for his spiritual and reformist pursuits, including hosting weekly meetings of the ‘Humanistic Club’.

It was donated to the Sri Aurobindo Society in 1979 and became a center for promoting the teachings of Sri Aurobindo and The Mother.
How to join this session
There is a meditation session that ends at 11:15 am, we have permission to draw at location after that. We start at 11:30am. We meet at the entrance for a quick briefing.
We have permission to draw outside the main Bhavan, drawing inside the building is not allowed.

📆 24th May 2026
⏱️ 11:30 am - 1:30 pm
📍 Sri Aurobindo Society

You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our community on Discord.
https://penciljam.com/main/community/

If this is your first time and would like future update on events, and more. Please register here.
Things to carry for this session

 	A sketchbook/paper/drawing surface
 	Pencils or any medium you would like to try out
 	A portable chair/mat. If the location has no seating, we tend to sit on the ground
 	Water bottle and snacks

How to get there
Follow the location map provided
This building is opposite Ulsoor Lake
Closest metro station: Trinity Circle Metro (1.2km walk)

name: Penciljam Bengaluru at MAP
description:
We are drawing at the Museum of Art and Photography this Sunday.

The Museum of Art & Photography (MAP), located in the heart of Bengaluru, is dedicated to making art accessible and engaging for all. Visit their website to see the current exhibits: https://map-india.org/

We are delighted to continue to partner with MAP who has made the space available for this session from 10:30am to 1pm. A quite day to immerse ourselves at the Museum.
How to join this session
You don't need artistic skill to participate in this event - just boundless curiosity and a love for drawing. To connect with other participants, please use our Discord community app. (Link to the Discord Community Here.)

Please register to attend this event. (Registration link will be updated here) Please arrive at the museum before 10:30 am to join the briefing & introduction for this session at the Museum.
Once you arrive at MAP

 	Inform the front desk that you are there for Penciljam,
 	Confirm your name against the RSVP list, you can also register on the spot for this event
 	Leave your bag in the lockers at the basement level.
 	You can carry an A4/A5 or smaller sketchbook and portable dry material kit to draw within.
 	Choose a spot you'd like to sketch and get comfortable - be mindful of not blocking the path of visitors to the museum.

Things to carry for this session:
-  A sketchbook/paper (A4/A5 or smaller sketchbook)

 	Art materials of your choice - Pencils or any dry medium. (within the museum we cannot use wet media like paints. Only non-messy dry media is allowed)

How to get there
This building is opposite the Government Museum.
Follow the location map provided
Closest metro station: Cubbon Park Metro station (900m from MAP)
